2026-04-20T02:15:41.2701501Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T02:15:41.2702025Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T02:15:41.2702447Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T02:15:41.2703274Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T02:15:41.2703578Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T02:15:41.2703811Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T02:15:41.2703987Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T02:15:41.2704178Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T02:15:41.2704381Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m

2026-04-20T02:19:51.5456413Z ◆ The `delete` keyword usage in the API file - this seems to be in object type definitions using `delete` as a property name (e.g., `delete: (...) => ...`). This is a known pattern where `delete` is a reserved word in JavaScript/TypeScript. However, since these are object properties (not standalone identifiers), this is actually valid TypeScript - `delete` can be used as a property name in an object literal or interface definition.
